title: Persistent Goal-Seeking Intelligence Accumulation Engine
short: The system accumulates capabilities, evidence, and economic value over time.
problem: Most AI work is episodic. It answers, acts, and disappears. A persistent intelligence system must remember what worked, under which constraints, and how it should influence future work.
mission: Demonstrate an experimental framework where GoalOS sets persistent aims, AGIALPHA coordinates proof work, Evidence Dockets preserve results, and selected artifacts accumulate into future operating context.
why: This is the transition from task automation to institutional intelligence. It defines how a system can compound capability rather than merely produce outputs.
plans: reuse selected artifact in next mission
agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates recurring proof work, settlement, credentialing, and reputation across a persistent mission history.
rsi: Recursive self-improvement becomes a controlled memory mechanism: selected artifacts may influence the next run; rejected artifacts cannot.
evidence: Red-team or challenge report where applicable
status: Experimental public proof-card dossier / evidence-docket template
plain_english: The system demonstrates persistent accumulation of capability, evidence, and value.
agent_roles: Converts institutional intent into a bounded mission
Designs the mission plan and evidence path
Produces the candidate artifact or workflow improvement
Runs the scorecard, benchmark, harness, or replay test
Packages artifacts into a proof bundle and evidence docket
Validates whether the proof should be accepted
Attempts to falsify the improvement and detect unsafe propagation
Grants or denies scoped upgrade rights
Routes verified value into future capability budget
Preserves what worked for future missions
skill_explanations: Goal decomposition
Used in this proof card for persistent goal-seeking intelligence accumulation engine: Turns a strategic objective into scoped proof missions with measurable outcomes.
Evidence packaging
Used in this proof card for persistent goal-seeking intelligence accumulation engine: Converts traces, artifacts, evaluator reports, and reviewer decisions into an inspectable docket.
Reviewer-ready documentation
Used in this proof card for persistent goal-seeking intelligence accumulation engine: Makes advanced work understandable to external reviewers without leaking private data.
Claim-boundary discipline
Used in this proof card for persistent goal-seeking intelligence accumulation engine: Prevents marketing claims from exceeding what the evidence supports.
Selection-gate reasoning
Used in this proof card for persistent goal-seeking intelligence accumulation engine: Determines whether an artifact earns scoped upgrade rights.
Rollback planning
Used in this proof card for persistent goal-seeking intelligence accumulation engine: Preserves a recovery path for rejected or unsafe changes.

plan_explanations: Define problem, sponsor, baseline, scope, risk, and desired proof artifact.
Route the mission to specialized cells with bounded responsibilities and outputs.
Predefine what evidence must be collected before work can be accepted.
Validate the claim, challenge the evidence, and record acceptance or rejection.
Allow only selected artifacts to influence future workflows.
Allocate verified value toward stronger future missions, audits, tooling, and capability.
Persistent Goal-Seeking Intelligence Accumulation Engine execution plan

Demonstrate an experimental framework where GoalOS sets persistent aims, AGIALPHA coordinates proof work, Evidence Dockets preserve results, and selected artifacts accumulate into future operating context.
goal_explanations: Proof completeness
Every promoted artifact must have a traceable Evidence Docket.
Operational usefulness
The result must improve a real buyer, sponsor, enterprise, or cyber-sovereign workflow.
Replayability
Independent reviewers must be able to inspect or reproduce the claim path.
Risk containment
Unsafe or unproven artifacts cannot become future operating context.
Economic discipline
AGIALPHA settlement must coordinate real proof work, not unsupported claims.
RSI efficiency
Future work should reuse what proved itself and avoid repeating rejected paths.
Accumulate Capability
Specific goal for this proof card: accumulate capability.
Preserve Institutional Memory
Specific goal for this proof card: preserve institutional memory.
Make Evidence Reusable
Specific goal for this proof card: make evidence reusable.
Prevent Unproven Context Propagation
Specific goal for this proof card: prevent unproven context propagation.
contract_explanations: AEPGoalOSCommitRegistry
Records the mission aim, authority, constraints, risk class, success criteria, allowed scope, and claim boundary.
AEPRunCommitmentRegistry
Binds the selected agents, tools, artifacts, context, budget, timeline, and execution plan before work begins.
JobRegistry
Publishes the proof mission so sponsors, builders, and reviewers can coordinate around a shared work object.
JobClaimBondManager
Requires accountable commitment from builders before they claim a mission.
ProofSubmissionRegistry
Records the proof submission hash, proof bundle URI, and public-safe evidence reference.
ReviewerBondRegistry
Requires reviewer accountability and supports challenge windows before reviewer bonds unlock.
ProofCardRegistry
Registers the public-safe Proof Card that makes the result inspectable without exposing private intelligence.
ProofCredentialRegistry
Issues non-transferable credentials to builders/reviewers who create validated proof work.
ReputationRegistry
Updates proof-backed reputation so future work can be routed to proven operators.
AEPEvidenceDocketRegistry
Anchors the evidence package: task, baseline, artifacts, proof, review, cost, risk, and replay metadata.
AEPSelectionGate
Decides whether an artifact earns scoped upgrade rights for future workflows.
AEPRollbackRegistry
Preserves rollback readiness so rejected or harmful changes can be reversed or contained.
AEPChronicleRegistry
Creates durable institutional memory of what worked, what failed, and what may be reused.
AlphaWorkUnitLedger
Measures proof-settled work units so capability, effort, validation, and settlement can be compared over time.
AEPRewardVault
Holds and releases AGIALPHA rewards according to proof, review, settlement, and challenge rules.
TreasuryRouter
Routes verified value toward future proof missions, audits, tools, security, and capability development.
PersistentAimRegistry
Supports the proof-card lifecycle by anchoring commitments, proof, review, selection, settlement, or memory.

evidence_checklist: GoalOSCommit
A signed/scoped mission objective with success criteria and claim boundary.
RunCommitment
The planned agents, tools, artifacts, context, cost limits, and evaluation route.
Candidate artifact
The workflow, policy, remediation, benchmark, or operating artifact being improved.
Evaluator report
A scorecard, benchmark, harness, or replay result comparing baseline and candidate.
ProofBundle
The hash-linked package of inputs, outputs, traces, and evaluation evidence.
Reviewer attestation
A bonded reviewer decision stating whether the evidence supports the claim.
Falsification / red-team note
A challenge report for reward hacking, unsafe propagation, or unsupported claims.
SelectionCertificate
The decision granting or rejecting scoped upgrade rights.
RollbackReceipt
The recovery path if a promoted artifact fails, regresses, or becomes unsafe.
ChronicleEntry
The durable memory record used by future missions.
faq: Does this claim achieved AGI or superintelligence?
No. It is an experimental proof-card framework for evidence-governed capability accumulation.
Public-safe hashes, proof-card summaries, claim boundaries, and selected evidence references.
Private data, sensitive workflows, security details, buyer data, and confidential operational context.
Only artifacts that survive proof, evaluation, review, and selection may shape future work.
AGIALPHA coordinates mission budgets, bonds, settlement, proof-card actions, credentials, and reputation-linked access.
glossary: A public-safe page explaining a proof mission, evidence standard, claim boundary, and upgrade logic.
The structured evidence package behind a proof card.
The decision layer that grants or denies upgrade rights.
The earned right for an artifact to influence future work.
Institutional memory of missions, proofs, decisions, and outcomes.
